methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 16 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.8x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Andromeda Surgical is a seed-stage biotech company founded by Nick Damiano and Kartik Tiwari that has raised $10M+. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 3 public companies (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private firms in the Biotech & Health sector, though narrow peer matching proved constrained.

Of three valuation methods examined, only stage-progression proved applicable. This method uses the $10M raised as a base proxy and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage progression, yielding an implied valuation of $25M. Peer-median analysis was not executable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation tiers; while 16 same-sector peers exist, none satisfied the minimum threshold of three datapoints in filtered cohorts. Public-comp anchoring was inapplicable because the target's revenue—required for P/S multiples—is not disclosed; the Biotech & Health public median stands at 7.8x P/S across the three tickers, but cannot be applied without this metric.

The triangulated range is $21M–$29M with a median of $25M. This estimate carries material uncertainty given reliance on a single method. Key risks include the appropriateness of the 2.5x step-up multiplier for Andromeda's specific stage and technology profile, and the lack of revenue disclosure that would enable public-market anchoring. Additional financial or milestone data would strengthen conviction.
